block_01: A series of events have taken place that boosted the profitability of certain assets.
block_02: The expected return is one of the key metrics to follow since it reflects the efficiency of your investments in an asset. The highest returns at the end of 2021 — up to 95% — may be obtained by traders who invest in Big Tech and entertainment industry stocks, as well as in oil and crypto.
block_03: Here’s a recap of the high profitability assets*
block_06: Bitcoin hit another record high of $65,000 and continued rising in price amid recent news on BTC futures ETF adoption. The launch of the first Bitcoin ETF is important because it is a fully regulated instrument that allows investing in cryptocurrencies.
block_08: The launch of the Bitcoin ETF is one of the most important milestones of 2021. It might trigger a large influx of institutional investors into the cryptocurrency market — those who previously refrained from crypto investing because of insufficient regulation and a low level of trust. Now, the barrier between large investors and cryptocurrencies might be overcome.
block_12: This fall, Apple held two product events and released renewed iGoodies with enhanced features, including more powerful chipsets.
block_14: The last "holiday" quarter of the year is always the most profitable for Apple since many people buy its devices as gifts. As a result, Apple expects to get a quarterly revenue of 84.8 billion (+31%) — time to make a move!
block_18: Netflix reported decent third-quarter results as subscriber growth beat the low guidance issued a quarter ago. Over the past quarter, the studio has released a number of mega-successful projects, including the South Korean ‘Squid Game’ — it was watched by two out of three users of the service.
block_20: In winter, people spend more time at home and use streaming services more often. In addition, Netflix announced a test launch of video games in several countries. Netflix shares' prognosed value increased to $666 apiece, the recommendation remains the same: "Buy".
block_24: The energy market crisis, which began with the rise in gas prices in Europe, has not yet been overcome. Gas prices remain high around the world, and this is one of the main drivers of the rise in oil prices: Crude Oil Brent hit $86 for the first time in a while, WTI is traded at $85.
block_26: Anticipation of a cold November increased tension on the gas market. According to some analysts, oil demand will recover to pre-pandemic levels only by the late summer of 2022.
block_28: *Information regarding past performance is not a reliable indicator of future performance
block_29: Up to 95% on highly profitable assets
